NVDA.reasoning: TREND ASSESSMENT: Daily EMA alignment is fully bullish (EMA-10 203.99 > EMA-21 197.67 > EMA-50 190.52 > EMA-200 177.38) and ADX at 55.96 is the strongest trend reading in recent pipeline history — confirming a robust uptrend structure. Price has pulled back 7.97% from the $216.83 swing high to $199.57, now sitting just above the 21 EMA (197.67) and approaching the Fib 38.2% level (196.75), creating a textbook EMA-pullback entry zone. RSI-14 at 59.74 and RSI-21 at 67.88 are healthy and non-stretched. MOMENTUM ASSESSMENT: Mixed — 21d ROC +14.43% confirms the strong prior trend, 10d ROC +0.62% is barely positive, and 5d ROC is -0.04% (essentially flat), indicating momentum is in mid-deceleration during the pullback, not yet re-accelerating. MACD histogram is positive (0.73) but bullish crossover is false. The strategy rule is to wait for re-acceleration, which has not fully occurred on daily timeframe. HOURLY WARNING: Hourly indicators are decisively bearish — minus_di 38.45 vs plus_di 11.92, MACD histogram -1.95, all hourly ROCs negative (-0.89, -4.73, -7.89), price below hourly EMA-24 and EMA-48. This hourly-vs-daily divergence is a significant caution flag per strategy rules. However, hourly RSI-21 at 23.58 is deeply oversold, suggesting the intraday selling may be approaching exhaustion near the 21 EMA / Fib 38.2% confluence. WIKI LESSONS: Identical EMA pullback Fib dip-buy setup failed yesterday (stop triggered at $205.30, -$264.65) — the prior entry at $209.50 was too early in the pullback. Today's price is ~$10 lower near stronger structural support (21 EMA + Fib 38.2% confluence at 196.75-197.67 vs the 23.6% level yesterday). Confidence reduced due to: (1) 5d momentum still decelerating, (2) hourly trend bearish, (3) same setup failed yesterday. Entry is contingent on price stabilizing at the 21 EMA zone and showing an intraday reversal candle — not a blind entry at current price. MACRO: Risk-on regime (S&P at ATH, AI capex intact per Alphabet/Amazon), but Iran geopolitical tail risk remains. Net bullish bias is warranted with the caveat that momentum must re-accelerate before full confidence.
